The Materials Project (MP) supports using artificial intelligence (AI) tools, such as large language models (LLMs) as tools for coding.
"AI" used in this document will refer to existing and future tools pending update of this policy.
However, contributors remain responsible for any of their published code, and the MP is responsible for any code that the MP merges and releases.

AI may not be used to generate comments when communicating with maintainers. 
The MP may flag or hide comments that we suspect to be AI-written.

As the MP conducts operations primarily in English, the use of AI tools in translating user comments is permitted, **provided that** users verify the fidelity of the AI-generated text.
When possible, include both the text in your language, and the AI-translated text.

When a user submits an issue, bug report, pull request, etc., the MP expects the user to be able to describe their contribution in their own words, in detail.
Users should be able to respond to comments and questions from maintainers in the MP.
Users who do not need the aid of AI translation tools **must paraphrase and may not copy and paste AI-generated responses** when posting comments, including responses to maintainer comments.

**The MP forbids explicitly the contributions of autonomous AI agents.**
All contributions (issues and pull requests) that cannot be verified to be human will be closed.

As the MP is a scientific group, any AI-generated content (comments, images, etc.) should be explicitly marked as quotations (using the `>` markdown syntax) and include relevant metadata such as the model(s) used in generating the content.
Users should be cognizant of maintainers' time, and edit any overly-long AI-generated text quotations.
